Article: Metaphorical Conception Within Episode 10
[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 23, 2010 11:17 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: STUFF LIKE MAGMA DIVER AND JET ALONE MAKE EVA WHAT IT IS

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Thu Sep 23, 2010 3:34 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: That, and episode 10 breaks away from the traditional phallic symbols to present to us the vaginal design of the volcano's mouth as some white matter (Eva Unit 02 in a white suit) enters into it to retrieve an embryo from it's depths. Not quite sure what that has to do in the grand scheme of the subtext. (Except maybe that sexual reproduction from this point on before Third Impact is pointless")

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Sep 23, 2010 4:23 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: That, and episode 10 breaks away from the traditional phallic symbols to present to us the vaginal design of the volcano's mouth as some white matter (Eva Unit 02 in a white suit) enters into it to retrieve an embryo from it's depths. Not quite sure what that has to do in the grand scheme of the subtext. (Except maybe that sexual reproduction from this point on before Third Impact is pointless.)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 23, 2010 7:58 pm Yeah... I know. That was a stretch. But hey, if buildings rising from the ground can be phallic symbols, then this sure as heck could be a vaginal reference. [/sarcasm] The only useful thing episode 10 really does is introduce Misato's scar so we know what to look for in episode 11 and explain why we never go after the Angels ever again. Again, only the most obvious for out action arc. That, and question why Eva Unit 01 didn't burn up when Shinji reached down for Asuka. But at least it helps take you completely off guard when they pull the rug out from underneath and reveal the series' darker side.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Sep 23, 2010 9:01 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: That, and episode 10 breaks away from the traditional

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Thu Sep 23, 2010 11:00 pm ran1 wrote: In regard to your dilemma, show Death and Rebirth, mindfiuck her, and draw her into the series, definitely. It's an odd way to go about it, but I have to give Muggy credit for the idea, it fits right into her tastes. Here's hoping your _____ fits right into her _______ as well, brah. If deep gets you two a hard-on for each other, then fuck yeah show her Eva. I was able to do the same with

Article: Metaphorical Conception Within Episode 10
 fits right into her tastes. Here's hoping your _____ fits right into her _______ as well, brah. If deep gets you two a hard-on for each other, then fuck yeah show her Eva. I was able to do the same with K-dramas quite recently, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Sep 24, 2010 3:03 am ran1 wrote: Oh, Xard, goddamnit here you go again. Bakemonogatari was deep in the positive sense. Here you go again with the arbitrary calling card and it not realizing Bake's brilliant use of extreme wide angles at high points of emotion to distance the viewer from its characters even more so than they already are. Its the animated equivalent to Barry Lyndon and it's fucking brilliant and ARRRGGHHHHHNNNNNGHHHHH.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 24, 2010 8:53 am Darkman.exe213 wrote: Are you implying that Asuka is a guy? Not saying that it's much of a stretch, but...yeah.
